description_long: This utility pole is a tall, cylindrical structure made primarily of wood, supporting a horizontal crossarm near its top. Multiple electrical wires are attached to the crossarm, running parallel to the ground. Two large cylindrical electrical transformers are mounted on one side of the pole, with visible brackets securing them in place. A single streetlight fixture extends outward from the pole below the transformers, featuring a curved arm and a lamp head. The pole exhibits a weathered, brownish surface with metallic and plastic components on the attached equipment. The overall design is typical for urban or suburban power distribution and street lighting.
description: A tall wooden utility pole with a horizontal crossarm, two mounted transformers, and an attached streetlight fixture.
description_view_0: Front view showing both transformers and the streetlight arm extending to the left.
description_view_1: Side view with both transformers visible, but the streetlight arm is not visible.
description_view_2: Rear angled view with one transformer and the streetlight arm extending to the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the back of the pole, both transformers, and the streetlight arm partially visible.
attribute_arrangement_of_crossarms: A single horizontal crossarm is mounted near the top of the pole.
